title: Brookline voters overwhelmingly approve tax override to avoid school, public safety cuts
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Brookline voters approved Question 1 tax override, with 8,859 yes votes and 5,826 no votes.
The override provides $23 million in additional revenue, preventing cuts to public safety and school positions.
Voter turnout was 35%, the highest recorded for a local election in Brookline, according to town results.

title: Do the Bruins have a potential No. 1 center already on their roster?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins seek a true No. 1 center with Cam Neely acknowledging the team's current lack of one.
Fraser Minten and James Hagens are young prospects seen as potential top-line centers for the Bruins' future.
Bruins may explore trades or wait for internal growth to fill the center role amid a competitive Atlantic Division.

title: Maine police arrest victim’s boyfriend for 1987 murder
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Maine police arrested Stephen Bouchard for the 1987 murder of his girlfriend, Alice Hawkes, nearly 40 years after her death.
Bouchard, 63, was dating and living with Hawkes in Westbrook, Maine when she was found dead in their apartment.
A reinvestigation by Maine's Major Crimes Unit in 2025 led the Attorney General's Office to seek murder charges against Bouchard.

title: Is Patriots’ 2026 schedule actually easier than many expected? This win-projection metric says so.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Sharp Football Analysis ranks the Patriots' 2026 schedule as the 12th-easiest in the NFL.
New England plays eight teams from last year's playoffs, including the Seahawks, Jaguars, and Steelers.
Matchups against teams like the Jets and Raiders contribute to the Patriots' easier schedule projection.

title: Could Patriots reunite with Stefon Diggs? One Patriots writer doesn’t expect it.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Stefon Diggs, a free agent, is looking for NFL teams after his release from the Patriots in March.
Patriots writer Phil Perry doubts a Diggs return, citing possibilities of an A.J. Brown trade.
Diggs had a successful 2025 with the Patriots but faced personal issues and was released for cap space.

title: Man, 31, killed in New Bedford shooting
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Steven Coan, 31, was found with multiple gunshot wounds inside his car in New Bedford and pronounced dead at a hospital.
The shooting occurred around 11:30 p.m. on May 6 near Acushnet Avenue and Russell Street, detected by a ShotSpotter.
The incident is being investigated as a homicide by State Police and New Bedford police, as announced by the DA.

title: Hayden, Rollins, and third Suffolk DA candidate Champion secure enough signatures to appear on ballot
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kevin Hayden, Rachael Rollins, and Linda Champion will appear on the Suffolk DA primary ballot after gathering enough signatures.
Hayden, Champion, and Rollins collected more than the required 1,000 signatures in Boston, Revere, Chelsea, and Winthrop.
The Suffolk DA primary election on September 1 will narrow the race to two candidates for the November general election.

title: These are Boston’s highest-paid tech CEOs, BBJ data shows
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Wayfair CEO Niraj Shah topped Boston's highest-paid tech CEOs in 2025 with $280.9 million in compensation.
Vincent Roche of Analog Devices ranked second with a total compensation of $27.5 million in 2025.
Third on the list was Neil Barua of PTC Inc., earning $24.3 million, as per the Boston Business Journal.

title: Karen Read’s SUV heads to auction in Woburn. Here’s what readers think it’s worth.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The 2021 Lexus LX 570 linked to Karen Read's case will be auctioned in Woburn on May 14.
Boston.com readers estimate the car might sell for under $70,000 to over $500,000, citing its notorious history.
Despite prior damages, the SUV is valued at $55,180 to $63,880 by Kelley Blue Book for similar condition vehicles.

title: ‘What happened today cannot stand’: Memorial Drive shooter fired more than 50 rounds before trooper, civilian intervened, DA says
author: Abby Patkin and Molly Farrar
bullets: Shooter Tyler Brown fired over 50 rounds on Memorial Drive, injuring two men, before being stopped.
A State Police trooper and Marine veteran civilian intervened, shooting Brown, who was later arrested.
Brown faces charges for armed assault with intent to murder; the incident posed no ongoing public threat.
